description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the openjdk:8 Docker image and defines three stages: build, dist, and tag. Add a build job in the build stage that runs `export GRADLE_USER_HOME=.gradle/` then `./gradlew clean check shadowJar -Pversion=$VERSION`, caches the .gradle/caches and .gradle/wrapper directories with key $CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG, excludes runs on tags, and always publishes the build/ directory as an artifact that expires in 1 hour. Add a dist job in the dist stage that also runs `export GRADLE_USER_HOME=.gradle/` and `./gradlew shadowDistZip -Pversion=$VERSION`, uses the same cache, runs only on the master branch (excluding tags), and uploads build/distributions as an artifact on success. Add a tag job in the tag stage that depends on the dist job, runs only on master, and executes a curl POST to the GitLab API to create a tag named $VERSION pointing at $CI_COMMIT_SHA using $GIT_PRIVATE_TOKEN and $GIT_PROJECT_ID (command: curl --header "PRIVATE-TOKEN: $GIT_PRIVATE_TOKEN" --header "Content-Type: application/json" -X POST -d '{"tag_name":"'$VERSION'", "ref":"'$CI_COMMIT_SHA'"}' https://gitlab.com/api/v4/projects/$GIT_PROJECT_ID/repository/tags 2>&1 | tee /dev/stderr | grep "$VERSION"). Define variables GIT_PROJECT_ID and VERSION (VERSION = 1.6.$CI_PIPELINE_IID).
